channel.title: Solana on Saurabh's personal site
channel.description: Recent content in Solana on Saurabh's personal site

item.title: Web3’s Real Attack Surface: Beyond Smart Contracts
item.description:
This notice is issued on behalf of Resolv Digital Assets Ltd. in relation to the Resolv protocol.

Earlier today, a malicious actor gained unauthorized access to Resolv infrastructure through compromised private key, resulting in the minting of approximately $80M of…

— Resolv Labs (@ResolvLabs) March 22, 2026

Once again its proven by the black hats that they don’t care how secure your codebase is or how many audits you have done.

There was no reentrancy. No oracle manipulation. No overflow hiding in an unchecked block. The contracts behaved exactly as designed. And yet, funds were drained.

item.title: Fuzz solana programs like a Pro - Part 1
item.description:
Smart contract exploits rarely come from obvious code paths. They emerge from unexpected edge case sequences of state transitions that traditional tests never explore. Most of the smart contracts today rely heavily on unit tests. Unit tests validate correctness along expected flows. Attackers don’t follow expected flows. This is where fuzzing becomes indispensable.

In this article, we’ll walk through how to write real, invariant-driven fuzz tests using Trident, a purpose-built fuzzing framework for Solana programs.
